title: buildah bud: COPY from symlink uses link target as destination name
body:
**Description**

When `buildah bud` processes a `COPY` command that specifies a symlink as its source, buildah will create the copy under the link target’s file name instead of under the link’s name. This only seems to arise when the link name bears a recognized archive suffix.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

```shell
cd $(mktemp -d)
touch archive.tar.gz
ln -s archive.tar.gz archive-link.tar.gz
printf 'FROM alpine
COPY archive-link.tar.gz .
RUN ls -l && stat archive-link.tar.gz
' >Dockerfile
buildah bud .
```

**Describe the results you received:**

A file named `archive.tar.gz` is created.

**Describe the results you expected:**

A file named `archive-link.tar.gz` is created.

**Additional information**

Note that when the the symlink is named `archive-link` with no file suffix instead (or when a non-archive suffix is used), then the `COPY` results in the expected name, i.e., `archive-link`.

**Output of `pacman -Qi buildah`:**

```
Name            : buildah
Version         : 1.15.1-1
Description     : A tool which facilitates building OCI images
Architecture    : x86_64
URL             : https://github.com/containers/buildah
Licenses        : Apache
Groups          : None
Provides        : None
Depends On      : runc  skopeo  slirp4netns
Optional Deps   : None
Required By     : None
Optional For    : None
Conflicts With  : None
Replaces        : None
Installed Size  : 27,42 MiB
Packager        : Morten Linderud <foxboron@archlinux.org>
Build Date      : Fr 31 Jul 2020 20:50:22 CEST
Install Date    : So 09 Aug 2020 17:28:17 CEST
Install Reason  : Explicitly installed
Install Script  : No
Validated By    : Signature
```

**Output of `buildah version`:**

```
Version:         1.15.1
Go Version:      go1.14.6
Image Spec:      1.0.1-dev
Runtime Spec:    1.0.2-dev
CNI Spec:        0.4.0
libcni Version:  v0.7.2-0.20190904153231-83439463f784
image Version:   5.5.1
Git Commit:      c967e88e
Built:           Fri Jul 31 20:50:22 2020
OS/Arch:         linux/amd64
```

**Output of `cat /etc/*release`:**

```
Arch Linux release
LSB_VERSION=1.4
DISTRIB_ID=Arch
DISTRIB_RELEASE=rolling
DISTRIB_DESCRIPTION="Arch Linux"
NAME="Arch Linux"
PRETTY_NAME="Arch Linux"
ID=arch
BUILD_ID=rolling
ANSI_COLOR="38;2;23;147;209"
HOME_URL="https://www.archlinux.org/"
DOCUMENTATION_URL="https://wiki.archlinux.org/"
SUPPORT_URL="https://bbs.archlinux.org/"
BUG_REPORT_URL="https://bugs.archlinux.org/"
LOGO=archlinux
```

**Output of `uname -a`:**

```
Linux unruh 5.7.12-arch1-1 #1 SMP PREEMPT Fri, 31 Jul 2020 17:38:22 +0000 x86_64 GNU/Linux
```

**Output of `cat /etc/containers/storage.conf`:**

```
[storage]
driver = "overlay"
runroot = "/var/run/containers/storage"
graphroot = "/var/lib/containers/storage"
[storage.options]
additionalimagestores = []
size = ""
override_kernel_check = "true"
mountopt = "nodev"
[storage.options.thinpool]
ostree_repo = ""
skip_mount_home = "false"
```

comments.body: This appears to arise from `imagebuildah/stage_executor.go:490`, where `.tar.gz` is special-cased so that the file-name part of the copy destination is removed. Presumably, this is just to support `ADD`, not `COPY`, so I wonder whether this should be conditional on the type of command.
